name: NamibRand Nature Reserve
description: The first IDA International Dark Sky Reserve in Africa. Sky Quality Meter readings above 22 mag/arcsec.

name: Aoraki Mackenzie Reserve
description: New Zealand's first dark sky reserve, centered on Aoraki/Mount Cook National Park.

name: Atacama Desert
description: One of Earth's driest places, home to some of the world's major observatories.

name: Warrumbungle National Park
description: Australia's first Dark Sky Park, home to Siding Spring Observatory.

name: Pic du Midi de Bigorre
description: Historic Pyrenean observatory, a rare dark sky oasis in Western Europe.

name: Cherry Springs State Park
description: Pennsylvania's premier stargazing destination, surrounded by Susquehannock State Forest.

name: Brecon Beacons
description: The fifth International Dark Sky Reserve in the world and the first in Wales.

name: Exmoor National Park
description: England's first dark sky reserve, on the border of Somerset and Devon.

name: Teide National Park
description: UNESCO World Heritage site with exceptional astronomical conditions.
